doctor_description: Subpleural ground-glass nodule measuring 7 mm in the LLL. Impression: GGN in LLL; per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, CT in 6–12 months to confirm persistence, then CT every 2 years until 5 years.
validator_description: Against background of gravitational (dependent) changes, subpleural ground-glass opacities in the left lung base and lingular segment; explicitly states no pulmonary nodules require follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations. Impression: subpleural GGOs may represent inflammatory/infectious disease.
management_impact: Doctor generated a Fleischner-compliant CT surveillance recommendation (6–12 months, then q2y × 5 years) for an opacity the validator attributes to gravitational/dependent change and infectious aetiology. This triggers unnecessary serial imaging.
notes: The validator explicitly rejects the nodule interpretation and attributes the LLL and lingular GGOs to dependent attenuation and possible infection/inflammation. Doctor's 7mm GGN call is an overcall that changes management by mandating CT follow-up. Grade 4 (significant overreport). Measure 364 not applicable — validator's conclusion is no nodule requiring follow-up.

anatomical_location: Left lower lobe subpleural / lingular segment
doctor_description: Mild bronchiectasis in the subpleural portions of the left lower lobe.
validator_description: Mild bronchiectasis in the subpleural portions of the left lower lobe and lingular segment.
management_impact: Doctor omits lingular segment involvement; minor additional extent, no change in management.
notes: Validator extends bronchiectasis to lingual segment as well. Clinically minor omission — same overall finding, no management change.

anatomical_location: Bilateral lungs (predominantly lower lobes, middle lobe, lingual segments)
doctor_description: Small areas of consolidation and mild ground-glass opacity in the RUL. Mosaic attenuation pattern, traction bronchiectasis, interlobular septal thickening predominantly in upper lobes. Impression: CT findings favor bronchopneumonia in the right upper lobe.
validator_description: Bilateral diffuse mosaic attenuation due to bronchial obstruction; walls of lower lobe, middle lobe and lingual bronchi severely thickened; subsegmental bronchi obstructed by contents (some calcified); centrilobular nodules and centrilobular GGOs in upper lobes. Impression: Diffuse bronchial disease — obliterating bronchiolitis or diffuse panbronchiolitis. Distal airways disease (centrilobular opacities).
management_impact: Bronchopneumonia is typically treated with antibiotics and short-term follow-up. Obliterating bronchiolitis / diffuse panbronchiolitis requires bronchodilators, long-term macrolide therapy (azithromycin), and pulmonology referral. Mischaracterisation would lead to different treatment and specialist referral — management is significantly different.
notes: This is a fundamental diagnostic discrepancy. Doctor describes a focal RUL process and labels it bronchopneumonia. Validator identifies a diffuse bilateral obstructive airways disease pattern (obliterating bronchiolitis/panbronchiolitis) affecting lower lobes, middle lobe and lingual segments bilaterally with severely thickened bronchi and obstructed subsegmental bronchi — a distinct clinical entity with different management. Grade 3.

validator_description: Calcification of the mitral and aortic valves.
management_impact: Mitral valve calcification is an incidental finding; does not change management in isolation but is a clinically relevant finding to document. No urgent management change.
notes: Doctor notes aortic valve calcification but omits mitral valve calcification. Validator reports both. Minor clinical omission — no immediate management change.

validator_description: L1 retrolisthesis, 4 mm, grade I according to Meyerding classification.
management_impact: Grade I retrolisthesis is a clinically relevant incidental finding that should be communicated to the referring clinician, but does not typically alter immediate management. Orthopedic/spine follow-up may be considered but is not emergent.
notes: Doctor completely omits L1 retrolisthesis. Validator grades it Meyerding I with 4mm displacement. This is a reportable incidental finding missed by the doctor. No acute management change, so Grade 2b rather than 3.

doctor_description: Coronary calcification, qualitatively moderate. Patient advised to consult PCP for preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment.
validator_description: Coronary calcification, qualitatively moderate. Patient advised to consult PCP for preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment.
notes: Both reports concordant on CAC severity and PCP recommendation. ACRad44 satisfied by both.

validator_description: 3 mm solid nodule in the upper lobe of the left lung (series 4, image 78), probably an intrapulmonary lymph node. No follow-up required per Fleischner Society recommendations.
management_impact: 3mm solid nodule attributed to intrapulmonary lymph node — validator explicitly states no follow-up required per Fleischner. Doctor's report already states 'No nodules requiring follow-up identified,' so clinical management is the same. Omission of specific nodule characterization is a minor clinical discrepancy.
notes: Doctor's report says no nodules requiring follow-up without mentioning the 3mm nodule. Validator identifies and characterises it but reaches same management conclusion (no follow-up needed). The omission is a documentation gap rather than a management-changing miss. Grade 2b.

anatomical_location: Thoracic and lumbar spine (T11, T12, L1, L2)
validator_description: Decreased vertebral body density: T11 - 105 HU, T12 - 123 HU, L1 - 106 HU, L2 - 111 HU. Impression: Decreased vertebral body density consistent with osteopenia.
management_impact: Osteopenia is an actionable incidental finding — may prompt DXA scan referral and calcium/vitamin D supplementation counseling. Doctor completely omits this finding. Does not change acute management but is a clinically relevant missed incidental finding.
notes: Doctor makes no mention of reduced vertebral bone density or osteopenia, despite the validator measuring HU values at four levels and flagging osteopenia in the impression. This is a meaningful clinical omission for a patient who may benefit from further bone density evaluation. Grade 2b — management referral (DXA) is not emergent.

doctor_description: Pulmonary trunk measures up to 3.7 cm — mentioned in findings but not commented upon; no mention of pulmonary hypertension in impression.
validator_description: Pulmonary trunk measures up to 3.7 cm. Impression: Dilated main pulmonary artery (37 mm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.
management_impact: Doctor records the 3.7cm PA measurement in findings but fails to flag it as dilated or comment on possible pulmonary hypertension in the impression. A dilated PA ≥3.6 cm is a recognised CT threshold suggesting pulmonary arterial hypertension, which would prompt echocardiography, pulmonology/cardiology referral, and further workup. This is a management-changing omission.
notes: Doctor includes the raw measurement (3.7cm) in the great vessels section but makes no interpretive comment. The impression ignores this entirely. Validator explicitly flags it as dilated and raises pulmonary hypertension. Given the patient also has cardiac chamber enlargement, this is a clinically significant omission that would change management (echocardiogram, specialist referral). Grade 3.

doctor_description: Moderate coronary calcification. No PCP referral recommendation in impression.
validator_description: Moderate coronary calcification. Impression includes PCP referral recommendation for preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment.
management_impact: Doctor documents moderate CAC in findings but omits the required PCP recommendation language in the impression. Validator includes the full ACRad44-compliant PCP referral statement. This is a MIPS documentation failure — the management recommendation itself (PCP referral) is missing.
notes: Doctor notes 'Moderate coronary calcification' in findings but the impression only says 'Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta. Moderate coronary calcification' — no PCP consultation recommendation. ACRad44 requires documentation of CAC severity plus PCP referral recommendation. The PCP recommendation is absent. Grade 2b (MIPS).

anatomical_location: Bilateral lungs (lower lobes bilateral, upper left lobe)
doctor_description: Solid nodules in segment 6 of right and left lungs, likely intrapulmonary lymph nodes. No Fleischner follow-up recommendation stated in impression.
validator_description: Solid nodules up to 4mm in lower right and left lobes, some possibly gravitational. Impression provides explicit Fleischner Society guidance: no follow-up needed for low-risk patients; optional CT at 12 months for high-risk with high index of suspicion.
management_impact: Doctor describes the nodules and attributes them to intrapulmonary lymph nodes but does not state the Fleischner follow-up recommendation in the impression. Validator provides the full Fleischner statement. No immediate management change, but the documentation is incomplete per Measure 364 requirements.
notes: Nodules are ≤6mm solid; Fleischner recommendation for this size is no follow-up (low risk) or optional CT at 12 months (high risk). Doctor implies no follow-up by calling them lymph nodes but does not state this explicitly in the impression. Measure 364 requires explicit statement of follow-up interval or 'no follow-up.' Grade 2b (MIPS).

validator_description: Mild calcifications of the aortic and mitral valves.
management_impact: Valve calcifications are incidental findings without immediate management change in this context. Clinically relevant to document.
notes: Doctor does not mention aortic or mitral valve calcifications; validator flags both as mild. Minor clinical omission, no management change.

validator_description: Subchondral cysts of the left shoulder joint.
management_impact: Subchondral cysts are degenerative incidental findings, no change in acute management.
notes: Minor incidental finding omitted by doctor. No management impact.

finding_category: Pulmonary emphysema — severity and LDCT screening
doctor_description: Mild centrilobular emphysematous changes in both lungs. Impression: Mild pulmonary emphysema.
validator_description: Moderate centrilobular emphysema in both lungs. Impression: Moderate pulmonary emphysema. Emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer — consider LDCT lung cancer screening enrollment; today's CT may serve as baseline.
management_impact: Doctor grades emphysema as mild; validator grades it as moderate and adds LDCT lung cancer screening recommendation (QMM23) which the doctor omits entirely. The indication states the patient is a current smoker. Moderate emphysema in a current smoker triggers LDCT eligibility assessment — an actionable management recommendation the doctor fails to provide.
notes: Two-part discrepancy: (1) mild vs moderate emphysema severity; (2) doctor omits LDCT screening recommendation despite patient being a current smoker with moderate emphysema, satisfying QMM23 criteria. The LDCT omission is the primary driver of Grade 3. QMM23 requires emphysema flagged as independent lung cancer risk factor + LDCT recommendation for eligible patients.

doctor_description: Coronary calcification, qualitatively mild. PCP referral recommendation included.
validator_description: Coronary calcification, qualitatively mild. PCP referral recommendation included.
notes: Both reports concordant on CAC severity (mild) and PCP recommendation. ACRad44 satisfied by both.

doctor_description: Small solid nodules in both lungs up to 4mm. Stable compared to prior CT. Impression: Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, additional CT follow-up is not recommended.
validator_description: Multiple centrilobular nodules predominantly in upper lobes up to 4mm, some with pseudocavity appearance. Without significant change. No follow-up required.
management_impact: No discrepancy in management recommendation — both agree no follow-up needed.
notes: Both reports agree on no follow-up for nodules. Validator adds description of pseudocavity appearance for some nodules (minor characterization). Overall concordant on management. Measure 364 satisfied by both.
